---
title: "Resources"
---


### Resources for further learning


**Python basics**
<br>
<https://bristol-training.github.io/intro-python-1/>
<br>
<https://bristol-training.github.io/intro-python-2/>

**Pandas and Seaborn**
<br>
<https://bristol-training.github.io/data-science-python-1/>

**Scikit-learn and machine learning**
<br>
<https://bristol-training.github.io/data-science-python-2/>
<br>
<https://bristol-training.github.io/decision-tree-random-forest/>


### AI in the NHS

Building a model that works on training data is the easy part. Deploying it safely — so that it improves outcomes rather than causing harm through automation bias, fairness failures, or poor calibration — requires multidisciplinary collaboration between clinicians, data scientists, information governance leads, and patients.

The [NHS AI Ethics Initiative](https://www.nhsx.nhs.uk/ai-lab/) and [Alan Turing Institute's guidance](https://www.turing.ac.uk/sites/default/files/2024-06/aieg-ati-8-accountabilityv1.2.pdf) are good starting points for understanding responsible deployment.

### Contact

For queries related to this course please contact [jgi-training@bristol.ac.uk](mailto:jgi-training@bristol.ac.uk).


If you are a PGR or resaerch staff at the University of Bristol (including Honorary contracts), you can get 8 hours of free data science support. Please contact [jgi-training@bristol.ac.uk](mailto:ask-jgi@bristol.ac.uk).


For collaborations or long term support, please contact [jgi-admin@bristol.ac.uk](mailto:jgi-admin@bristol.ac.uk).
